windows.embed_text_v2: measure temperature of melted wax with digital probe thermometer in black metal pouring pitcher, dark molten wax cooling to 185-187 degrees, clear cup and supply bottles nearby
windows.summary_v2: A digital probe thermometer monitors the cooling temperature of dark melted wax in a black pouring pitcher, reading in the mid-180s Fahrenheit.

tags_v2.setting: countertop, overhead, kraft paper bags in background
tags_v2.on_screen_text: thermometer display reads ~186 F; 'TP300' on probe; 'Hobby Lobby' on paper bags

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead view of a black metal pouring pitcher filled with dark melted wax; a white digital probe thermometer (TP300) stands in the wax reading about 187 degrees F. A small empty clear cup sits to the left, with kraft paper bags and supply bottles in the background.
Same black pitcher of melted wax; the thermometer probe reads roughly 186.9 degrees F as the wax cools.
The dark melted wax sits in the black pitcher, thermometer display steady around 186.8 degrees F.
Pitcher of molten wax with the thermometer reading near 186.0 degrees F; the empty clear cup and kraft bags remain at left.
Melted wax in the black pitcher, thermometer holding about 186.6 degrees F as it gradually drops.
Same setup; the probe thermometer now reads roughly 186.4 degrees F in the dark wax.
Black pitcher of molten wax, thermometer steady near 186.4 degrees F, no hands in frame.
The wax cools further with the thermometer reading about 186.0 degrees F inside the black pouring pitcher.
Melted wax in the black pitcher with the digital thermometer now reading roughly 185.x degrees F, monitoring the cooling temperature before pouring.

windows.embed_text_v2: measuring wax temperature with TP200 digital probe thermometer inside black aluminum pouring pitcher of melted dark paraffin wax cooling around 185 degrees Fahrenheit on grey countertop before pour
windows.summary_v2: Checking melted paraffin wax temperature with a digital probe thermometer in a black pouring pitcher, watching it cool toward pour temp.

tags_v2.on_screen_text: 185.7F / 185.5F / 184.7F on thermometer display; CHIPOTLE on paper bag

seconds_v2.desc: Black aluminum pouring pitcher with melted wax inside; a digital probe thermometer (TP200) reads 185.7 F. A small empty plastic cup sits at left and a brown kraft pouch labeled CHIPOTLE is in the background.
Same pouring-pitcher setup viewed from above; thermometer still reads 185.7 F as the maker monitors wax temperature before pour.
Thermometer probe in the same pitcher of dark melted wax now reads 185.7 F, steady.
Slight tick as thermometer reads 185.5 F; pitcher of wax sits on a grey countertop with fragrance pipette and beaker visible at edges.
Still reading 185.5 F on the thermometer's LCD inside the black pouring pitcher; setup unchanged.
Probe shows 185.3 F; the wax in the spouted aluminum pitcher cools slowly while the maker holds the thermometer in place.
Thermometer reads 185.0 F; same overhead shot of the black pouring pitcher with the temperature steadily dropping.
LCD now displays 184.7 F as the molten paraffin in the pitcher continues to cool before pouring.
Thermometer holds at 184.7 F inside the black pouring pitcher; small clear sample cup and Chipotle paper bag still visible nearby.

windows.embed_text_v2: measuring melted wax temperature with digital probe thermometer reading 183-187F in black pouring pitcher of dark wax, candle making workbench with measuring cups and bottles
windows.summary_v2: A digital probe thermometer monitors the temperature of dark melted wax cooling in a black pouring pitcher, showing readings around 183-187F on a candle-making workbench.

seconds_v2.desc: A digital probe thermometer stands upright in a black pouring pitcher of dark melted wax on a workbench, its screen reading roughly 187F as the wax temperature is checked.
The thermometer display now shows about 184F in the black wax-filled pitcher; an empty plastic cup, glass bottles, and a brown paper bag sit in the background.
Probe thermometer continues monitoring the melted wax, reading near 183-187F, the digital screen facing the camera above the black pouring jug.
Same setup, the thermometer in the dark wax shows roughly 187F as the wax slowly cools in the black pitcher on the cluttered counter.
The display reads about 187F; the probe stays submerged in the dark melted wax inside the black pouring pitcher.
Thermometer screen now shows around 183F over the black pitcher of melted wax, tracking the cooling temperature before fragrance or pouring.
The probe reads near 183F in the dark wax; the black pouring jug sits on the bench beside a measuring cup and bottles.
Digital thermometer continues to display roughly 183F in the melted wax inside the black pitcher.
The thermometer reads about 183F as the dark wax cools in the black pouring pitcher, temperature still being monitored.

windows.embed_text_v2: pouring molten red wax from black metal pitcher into clear glass jar with black two-prong wick centering holder and cotton wicks, on parchment paper, wax level rising, pink-red color
windows.summary_v2: A black pouring pitcher fills a wick-holdered glass jar with molten pink-red wax, the level rising as the pour continues.

tags_v2.setting: craft table with parchment paper, kitchen scale and kraft envelopes

seconds_v2.desc: A black metal pouring pitcher tips molten pink-red wax into a clear glass jar fitted with a black wick-centering bar holding two wicks; jar sits on parchment, scale and kraft envelopes behind.
Pouring continues, a thin stream of red wax runs from the pitcher spout into the jar past the centered wicks; wax level sits low in the jar.
Steady pour of liquid red wax into the glass jar; the black two-prong wick holder keeps the pale wicks upright and centered.
The pitcher pours more red wax; the pool inside the jar deepens and reddens around the wick base.
Pour ongoing, wax stream hits the surface causing slight ripples; jar roughly a third full of red wax.
Black pitcher held over the jar pouring red wax; level rising toward the middle of the glass.
Continued pour of molten red wax into the jar; wicks stay centered by the black holder as the wax climbs.
Pour nearly filling the jar, red wax now well past halfway up the glass container.
Final stream of red wax poured in; jar is mostly full, pitcher still tipped over the rim.

windows.embed_text_v2: finishing pour of red wax into glass jar candle with round black wick-centering holder and two cotton wicks, top-down view of glossy molten red wax surface, jar setting on parchment, cooling
windows.summary_v2: The pour is finished and the glass jar full of red wax is shown top-down and from the side as it begins to set with its wick holder in place.

tags_v2.action: finishing pour and setting jar candle
tags_v2.setting: craft bench with parchment paper and kraft envelopes

seconds_v2.desc: Black pouring pitcher finishes adding red wax to a glass jar topped with a round black wick-centering disc holding two pale wicks; jar nearly full on parchment.
Pitcher tips away as the last of the red wax settles in the jar; wicks held upright by the black disc, full surface of liquid red wax visible.
Overhead top-down view of the just-poured red wax surface inside the jar, ringed by the circular ridged black wick holder with two wicks crossing it.
Top-down view of the glossy deep-red molten wax pool in the jar; black wick-centering disc and two wicks frame the surface.
Angled overhead shot of the filled jar, glossy red wax surface with a warm reflection, black wick holder and wicks on top, jar setting on the bench.
Close overhead of the red wax surface beginning to settle in the jar; ridged black disc and pale wicks visible as wax cools.
Camera pulls back to show the full filled jar of red wax with black wick holder, sitting on parchment, wax now setting.
Side view of the poured red wax candle in its glass jar resting on the bench, wick holder and wicks on top, wax cooling.
Filled red-wax jar candle stands on the parchment surface, black wick-centering disc and wicks in place, the poured wax left to set.
